title: -Contract: Environmental Logistics, Inc. and Advanced Chemical Transport, LLC for On-Call Hazardous Waste Management

sections.recommendation:
Adopt two Resolutions authorizing the City Manager to:
1. Execute a three-year contract and any amendments with Environmental
Logistics, Inc. to provide on-call hazardous waste disposal at various City
facilities and locations for a total amount not to exceed $200,000 for an initial
three-year term from August 1, 2025, to August 1, 2028, with an option of two
one-year extensions, for a maximum term of five years. The service rates will be
adjusted annually in accordance with the Consumer Price Index and the terms of
the agreement.
2. Execute a three-year contract and any amendment with Advanced Chemical
Transport, LLC to provide on-call hazardous waste disposal at various City
facilities and locations for a total amount not to exceed $150,000 for an initial
three-year term from August 1, 2025, to August 1, 2028, with an option of two
one-year extensions, for a maximum term of five years. The service rates will be
adjusted annually in accordance with the Consumer Price Index and the terms of
the agreement.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The execution of both contracts for on-call hazardous waste management will not
exceed $350,000 in total. Annual expenditures are anticipated to be approximately
$115,000. Funding in the amount of $350,000 is available in the Zero Waste Fund
(Fund 601) FY2026 adopted budget. The available budget is sufficient to fund
scheduled mandatory hazardous waste disposal services. Additional costs may include
as needed hazardous waste management due to spill response or other emergencies.
Funding for future years is subject to Council’s approval of the proposed citywide
budget for Fiscal Years 2027-2028.

sections.current_situation:
The City requires hazardous waste management contract services at various locations
on an as-needed basis to recycle or dispose of both City generated and illegally
dumped waste. Contractors must have the experience, qualifications, and capacity to
collect, transport, and dispose of hazardous waste. The terms of these contracts include
hazardous waste characterization and disposal, as well as providing handling materials
for waste storage and transport.
On February 27, 2025, the City released Request for Proposal (RFP) #25-116705-C for
hazardous waste management services. The solicitation was open for fifty-four days
and the City received four responses. The evaluation criteria used to rate the proposals
were as follows: Expertise & References (30%), Cost (30%), and Firm Qualifications
and Experience (40%).
As a result of the competitive proposal solicitation that was conducted, Environmental
Logistics Inc. and Advanced Chemical Transport LLC were determined to be the most
responsive and responsible bidders. Both companies were highly recommended by
professional references and had competitive pricing for services. These agreements are
for a term of three years, with an option to extend by two, one-year extensions.
The On-Call Hazardous Waste Management Contracts are aligned with the City’s
Strategic Plan Priorities of advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-
maintained infrastructure, amenities, and facilities and to be a global leader in protecting
the environment.
sections.background:
The City is responsible for ensuring proper disposal of abandoned hazardous waste
found in the public right-of-way, illegally disposed of at the City’s Transfer Station, and
assisting in response to accidental spills or discharges on public property. In addition,
the Public Works Department coordinates the disposal of City generated hazardous
waste. The City also operates two public used oil take back programs; one at the City’s
Marina and the other at the City’s Transfer Station. The used oil from these programs
will be recycled by the contractor.
The hazardous waste contractor will provide proper clean-up, collection, storage,
handling, recycling, reuse, treatment, packaging, documentation, transportation,
transfer, and disposal of hazardous waste. The City does not operate a household
hazardous waste facility and directs residents and businesses to utilize the County’s
facilities. In addition, there may be special projects such as storage tank cleaning,
contaminated soil removal, and emergency response services requested of the
contractor.
Page 2
Page 3 of 5
Contract: Environmental Logistics and Advanced Chemical Transport CONSENT CALENDAR
for On-Call Hazardous Waste Management July 29, 2025
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
State and federal requirements for environmental compliance and sustainability ensure
minimal environmental impact from City activities. Public Works addresses these
through contracting with vendors like Environmental Logistics Inc. and Advanced
Chemical Transport LLC. Failure to manage hazardous waste could result in
contamination of soil, surface or groundwaters by used oil or other hazardous waste.
sections.rationale:
A contractor must be selected, as City staff do not have the equipment, capacity, or
appropriate training certifications to ensure compliance with state and federal
requirements for hazardous waste management. The selection of a contractor
additionally ensures that there are no negative environmental impacts from hazardous
waste that has been improperly disposed.
sections.alternatives:
No other alternative actions were considered due to the regulatory needs. The City
issued a solicitation for proposals and exercised due diligence in evaluating the bids to
satisfy the competitive process. Failure to execute these contracts will results in
compliance issues and exposure to regulatory fines.
CONTACT PERSON
Robert Hernandez, Environmental Compliance Specialist, Department of Public Works
(510) 981-6644
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, Department of Public Works (510) 981-6629

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: ENVIRONMENTAL LOGISTICS INC. FOR ON-CALL HAZARDOUS
WASTE MANAGEMENT
WHEREAS, hazardous waste management services are critical for regulatory compliance
at various city locations; and
WHEREAS, the City does not have appropriately trained and certified staff or equipment
to perform these services at these sites, which an outside vendor has traditionally served;
and
WHEREAS, no City employees are displaced by this contract; and
WHEREAS, on February 27, 2025, the City released RFP #25-116705-C for hazardous
waste management services. The solicitation was open for fifty-four days and the City
received four responses; and
WHEREAS, as a result of the competitive proposal solicitation that was held for providing
on-call hazardous waste management services to various city locations and facilities, both
Environmental Logistics Inc. and Advanced Chemical Transport LLC were determined to
be the most responsive and responsible bidders; and
WHEREAS, funding in the amount of $200,000 for the contract is available in Fiscal Year
2026 adopted budget in Zero Waste Fund 601; and
WHEREAS, additional funding for unforeseen emergency expenses or planned projects
will be appropriated as part of the budget process; and
WHEREAS, funding for budget allocations for future fiscal years are subject to adoption
of the budget by the Council.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizing
the City Manager to execute a contract with Environmental Logistics Inc. to provide on-
call hazardous waste management services at various city facilities and locations for a
total amount not to exceed $200,000 for an initial three-year term from August 1, 2025,
to August 1, 2028, with an option of two one-year extensions for a maximum term of five
years.
